title: C002: Add container image CI workflow
summary: Review #3 feedback from greptile-apps[bot] addressed: `fail-fast: false` added to both `build-service-images` and `smoke-test` matrix strategy blocks. Two review pattern files persisted capturing lessons learned (immutable tags, fail-fast). YAML validates, all 67 verification tests pass. Change is minimal (2 lines in workflow) and correctly scoped.

tests_checklist.item: Edge cases covered (tag derivation, patch counting, branch sanitization)
Security tests present (expression injection defense, fork PR rejection)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing ci.yaml workflow conventions (SPDX, quoted on, permissions, concurrency)
Change is minimal — only fail-fast: false added, no scope creep
Review pattern files well-structured with detection hints and examples

security_checklist.item: No expression injection risks (run blocks use env vars)
GITHUB_TOKEN permissions scoped per-job (packages: write only where needed)
Fork PRs rejected with clear error (read-only GITHUB_TOKEN cannot push)
Actions pinned to full 40-char commit SHAs

architecture_checklist.item: fail-fast: false correctly placed on both matrix jobs
Review patterns correctly capture lessons for future reviews
External review JSON accurately documents addressed feedback

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No unnecessary additions beyond review feedback

fail_fast_checklist.item: Errors detected early (fork PR rejection, timeout-minutes on all jobs)
Matrix failures no longer cancel siblings (fail-fast: false)

defensive_checklist.item: All external inputs validated (env vars for matrix values, hashFiles guard for patches)
Idempotent steps (apply-constraint-overrides.sh exits 0 when no overrides)

suggested_improvements: Minor: Consider adding a test assertion in verify_build_images_workflow.sh to verify fail-fast: false on matrix jobs (e.g., `yq '.jobs["build-service-images"]["strategy"]["fail-fast"]' == 'false'`). Currently the property is set but not verified by the test suite. Non-blocking since the review pattern documents the requirement.
